summary: REQ-9 requires FaceScanComponent to call navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ video: true }) immediately on mount via a useEffect with an empty dependency array, auto-start the video feed on permission grant, and show a clear error message on denial. The diff shows FaceScanComponent was updated with a useEffect (empty dep array) that calls requestCamera() on mount, which delegates to startCamera() via useCamera hook. It shows a permission-denied banner with instructions when error.type === 'permission'. The implementation satisfies all REQ-9 acceptance criteria. The constraint against modifying files under frontend/src/hooks/ appears respected (no hook files modified).
